rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF NORMA MOGANNAM
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #4, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 9/20/2022. Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. 1) The ending balance of assets in interim and final accounts filed by fiduciaries, who are not financial institutions, must be verified. Verification is made by original bank or brokerage statements or original bank letters signed by a bank officer with the authority to sign, showing the vesting of the account, the date, and the amount of the balance. Photocopies are not acceptable. See CRC 7.575(b) for computer-generated printouts. [LRSF 14.34 D.] 2) Please provide a revised proposed order to the Examiner that does not have "Episcopal Church and Abortion" printed on the back side of the proposed order. 3) Please file conservator timesheets that comply with LRSF 14.52. 4) On Schedule C - Disbursements please explain the entries titled Check Image Service. 5) Please explain the legal basis for why the conservator should not incur a fee reduction for overdraft charges. 6) Please explain the legal basis for not requiring Conservator to account for payments to Norma Mogannam. 7) Please file receipts and explain the $2,500.00 requested reimbursement to Jeffrey N. Mogannam. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 9/12/2022 [LRSF 14.7 B.]

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ROBERT PICASO
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order Determining Heirship
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 9/20/2022. Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. 1) The court cannot approve the petition based on information and belief please provide evidence in support of the petition. 2) The petition must include a family tree with supporting documentation of the heirs' relationships to the decedent. [LRSF 14.35 H.) 3) The petition does not include a request for who is entitled to distribution and instead requests on page 3 1) Determining disposition of the balance of the estate on hands and 2) For reimbursement of costs of suit incurred herein. Please file a memorandum of points and authorities in support of the request for 1) Determining disposition of the balance of the estate on hands and 2) For reimbursement of costs of suit incurred herein. 4) Please file certified copies of letters for Michelle Holmes, Administrator of Estate of Richard Holmes. 5) Petitioner must provide the required courtesy copy of the petition (examiner received an unfiled copy which has been recycled) and a proposed order. Petitioner must submit a hard copy of the proposed order to the Examiner in room 202 at least five (5) court days in advance of the scheduled hearing date. [LRSF 14.7]. Proposed orders dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the envelope or a cover page, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 9/6/2022 [LRSF 14.7 B.]

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF DAVID ZHANG
rulings.Calendar Matter: PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 10/19/22. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: The only correct form of notice in a decedent's estate is the MANDATORY Judicial Council form DE-120. If the proof of service on the reverse side of that form is not used, then a copy of the Notice of Hearing that was served must be attached to the pleading form of proof of service. Notice mailed before the filing (e-filing) of the pleading requiring notice is defective. [LRSF 14.14 A.] As noted in the tentative ruling for the 7/12/22 hearing, notice was to be given AFTER the amended pleading was filed. (The proof of service e-filed on 7/12/22 indicates that notice was mailed well before the amended pleading was e-filed.) Notwithstanding the amended petition still alleging that the administrator will require an additional six months from the date of the hearing of this petition -- nearly six months after her attorney first signed the initial "Status Report" stating the same -- delayed hearings due to procedural defects will not increase the amount of time for estate administration. An appearance will likely be required on 10/19/22 if the notice issue is not fully and timely addressed. A courtesy copy of the amended petition and the correct proof of service of notice of the 10/19/22 hearing of the amended petition must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 10/10/22. Courtesy copies are file stamped, single-sided, hard copies. Examiners will not review a document until after it has been filed and a courtesy copy has been received. [LRSF 14.7 C.] Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (The administrator was appointed on 9/1/20, not 9/2/20; the order appointing her was FILED on 9/2/22. Item 4 of the I&A should NOT be completed when a Probate Referee WAS appointed. The creditors' claims period expired on 1/2/21, four months after Letters were issued, not 2/8/21. A petitioner does not need to give notice to herself. [Probate Code Sect. 1201.])

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ALLAN J. CRICHTON
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing. Although a personal representative's statutory compensation includes services to prepare an accounting and any payment to a third party to prepare the court accounting should reduce the statutory compensation, in light of the personal representative and her attorney not seeking any compensation for extraordinary services for selling the real property, the administrator's statutory compensation will not be reduced. Furthermore, it is noted that she did not include the receipts of bank interest in the fee base. A hearing will be set for 2/28/23 for status of the $110,000 reserve.

rulings.Case Title: IN RE: KAZUKO MIYAMOTO 2020 LIVING TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition Of Alicia M. Gamez For Order Directing Payment For Attorney Fees And Costs
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. 1) The petitioner requests an order authorizing and directing the trustee to pay compensation for the petitioner's legal services rendered to the former temporary conservatee. The petitioner was not appointed by the Court to represent the former temporary conservatee. 2) The petitioner requests an order authorizing and directing the trustee to pay compensation for the petitioner's legal services rendered to the settlor and beneficiary of the trust. The trustee filed a joinder in the petition. The petitioner refers to Probate Code section 18200 to characterize herself as a creditor of the settlor of a revocable trust, but the petition was not brought under section 850(a)(3)(C). PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Counsel or parties appearing in person must wear a mask covering their nose and mouth at all times while in the courthouse. Please review LRSF 14.1(D) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MARY DAVIS
rulings.Calendar Matter: PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 09/12/2022.Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. The status report must be signed and veriifed by the personal representative. (CRC 7.103) Personal representative must give 15 days notice of the petition. Petitions need to include sufficient allegations to allow the Court to determine administration is proceeding properly. Please make all necessary allegations as if the petition was for final distribution, including but not limited to: Notice under Probate Code 9202; Creditor's Claims; Assignments/Powers of Attorney; Requests for Special Notice, etc. Petitioner must submit a hard copy of the proposed order to the Examiner in room 202 at least five (5) court days in advance of the scheduled hearing date. [LRSF 14.7]. Proposed orders dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the envelope or a cover page, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 9/6/2022 [LRSF 14.7 B.]

rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. The amended petition was not e-filed until 8/9/22 (when it was available for viewing), which was not at least 15 days before this hearing. The proof of publication of "Notice of Sale" filed on 8/16/22 shows that notice of this sale confirmation hearing was published, not the notice of intent to sell real property. [Probate Code Sections 10300 and 10304 and LRSF 14.31 B.] Due to COVID-19, counsel and parties should appear remotely through Zoom or CourtCall whenever possible. Please review LRSF 14.1 D. for information on remote appearances. For Zoom appearance: Meeting ID is 160 225 4765 and password is 514879. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. PARTIES APPEARING MUST CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING USING THEIR FULL NAME.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF LENNIE L. GAINES
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing. The Court chooses to ignore the erroneous published notice that indicates that a "second amended petition" was filed, when no such petition was filed. A second AMENDMENT TO the petition was filed. The information in that second amendment clarified the information about the decedent's former (divorced, not deceased) husband, which this Court also chooses to accept as a supplement to the petition, rather than an amendment, which would have required notice. The "second amendment" was not actually e-filed until 6/23/22, when it was first available for viewing, so mailing notice of it on 6/17/22 would have been defective, had the amendment been a truly substantive amendment. [LRSF 14.14 A.] Status of closing the estate is set for 9/5/2023. A petition for final distribution or a report of status with a petition requesting additional time for estate administration must be filed at least two weeks before 9/5/2023. Appearance will be required on 9/5/2023 if no petition has been filed. No copy of the order will be sent to the attorney, as no SASE was provided.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF SARAH BURNS-TURNER
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Counsel or parties appearing in person must wear a mask covering their nose and mouth at all times while in the courthouse. Please review LRSF 14.1(D) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE REVOCABLE TRUST DECLARATION OF JOE LOUIE GARCIA
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Instructions; To Determine Distribution Of Trust
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 10/04/2022. Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. 1) Please explain the legal basis for San Francisco being the proper venue. 2) The cited statute about Omitted Spouse/Child alleges "the omitted spouse shall receive a share in the decedent's estate" "the omitted child shall receive a share in the decedent's estate" Please explain and provide the legal basis for the court determining an omitted spouse / omitted child in a trust when the statute appears to require a decedent's estate. 3) Please file a declaration of diligent search as required under CRC 7.52 for Rene Garcia and Steve Garcia. 4) Please file evidence proving the alleged assets are community property and what assets are community property. 5) Petitioner must submit a hard copy of the proposed order to the Examiner in room 202 at least five (5) court days in advance of the scheduled hearing date. [LRSF 14.7]. Proposed orders dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the envelope or a cover page, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 9/26/2022 [LRSF 14.7 B.]

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ELANA LAUB
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 9/20/2022. Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. 1) In a declaration signed under penalty of perjury and verified by the proposed executor please explain why a petition for probate has been filed if the alleged assets are $0. The court is not inclined to move forward with a petition for probate when there are no alleged assets and no specific reason given for opening up this probate. 2) Please state the date of death for Uri David Laub. [See LRSF 14.18 D.1)] An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 9/6/2022 [LRSF 14.7 B.]

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF KEITH GABRIEL
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 10/19/22. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: No original will appears to have been lodged yet. No proposed order has been received by the assigned examiner. [LRSF 14.7 A. and 14.59 E.] No Acknowledgment of Receipt of the Duties & Liabilities has been filed yet. No courtesy copies. [LRSF 2.7 B. and 14.7 B. and C.] An initial review of the scanned petition indicates the following issues: "By Bond Services" in item 1 of the petition is not the name of a newspaper. A proposed non-resident personal representative must file a declaration setting forth in detail the anticipated liabilities of the decedent and claims against the estate for the Court to determine the amount of the bond. [LRSF 14.16 F.] Item 3f(2) of the petition appears to be incomplete. Item 6f of the petition does not appear to be the first box that applies if cousins survived. When second generation or more distant heirs are listed, the deceased ancestor through whom they take (or would have taken) shall be named and his or her date of death and relationship to the decedent shall be stated. [LRSF 14.18 C.] Who were the decedent's grandparents and who were their issue who were his parents and the parents of the cousins, and what were their dates of death, if they are all deceased? Whose child is Gilad Gabriel and why was notice sent to him in care of Gideon Gabriel? Notice must be sent directly to anyone entitled to notice. If a person entitled to notice is a minor, the actual age of the minor must be set forth and the person having legal custody of the minor with whom the minor resides must be identified in item 8 of the petition and must also receive notice. [CRC 7.51(d).] The proposed order and all courtesy copies, including of any papers filed to address these issues, must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 10/10/22. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF CATHERINE M. DUBROW
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 9/20/2022. Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. 1) Notice does not indicate a copy of the notice was served with a copy of the petition. Please give re-do notice and give 15 days' required notice and serve the documents. (See page 2 #5 of DE-121) 2) Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number (Judicial Council Form DE-147S) is also required, pursuant to Probate Code 8404(b). Form DE-147S must be filed with a cover page, separately, and confidentially. Do not file form DE-147S as an attachment to the Duties and Liabilities form. [LRSF 14.18 E.] 3) Petitioner must submit a hard copy of the proposed order to the Examiner in room 202 at least five (5) court days in advance of the scheduled hearing date. [LRSF 14.7]. Proposed orders dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the envelope or a cover page, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 9/12/2022 [LRSF 14.7 B.]
